tests: tests/integration/test_perf_k6_load.py::test_events_load_meets_ latency_and_error_budgets (k6 self-skips: k6 run failed to execute in this environment, rc=255)

tests/integration/test_usage_export_perf.py::test_export_p95_timing_meets_the_confirmed_3000ms_budget_at_the_row_cap (deselected by -m 'not perf' per M4P-10; unchanged pre-existing perf test, not part of this feature's diff)

tests_by_type_note: Counts are the NEW tests added for this feature: 25 unit tests in tests/test_schemas_usage_daily.py (parse_daily_date / day_window_for / DailyUsageQueryParams / DailyUsageResponse / DailyMetricCount validation-contract cases, including a 10-case parametrized malformed-date sweep), and 17 integration tests -- 15 in tests/integration/test_usage_daily_endpoint.py (AC1-AC15 end-to-end) plus 2 in tests/integration/test_usage_rollup_rls_backstop.py (the RLS defense-in-depth backstop for AC7). Full-suite composition (pre-existing + new) totals 259 collected (255 passed, 4 skipped/deselected). This matches the plan's declared pyramid strategy: pure validation/boundary logic stayed unit, while auth/tenant-isolation/rate-limit/log/DAST/fail-closed guarantees -- only real against actual Postgres/Redis -- are integration.

by_id.reason: Seeds usage_rollup rows across two metrics, two customer_ids, and two hour-buckets on the same UTC day; asserts summed per-metric event_count in metric order.
Queries a date with no events; asserts 200 with metrics == [] (never 404).
Integration wire-check plus unit test on parse_daily_date(None) both assert 400 bad_request, not 422/200.

Parametrized unit test covers 10 malformed forms (short month/day, slash format, invalid month/day, non-date string, empty, whitespace-padded, datetime-with-time, trailing junk) plus a separate calendar-invalid Feb 29-on-non-leap-year test; each raises 400. Integration test wires one form through the real endpoint end-to-end.

Both out-of-range boundaries (too old, too future) assert 400; both inclusive boundary edges (exactly -90d, exactly +1d) assert acceptance, proving the range is inclusive at the exact edge, not just generically 'out of range'.

An undeclared query param (api_key_id) is rejected 422 validation_failed via extra='forbid', distinct from the 400 date-value contract. Closes the F4-01 unknown-param class for this endpoint (verified this session -- confirmed the assertion exists and targets the correct status/code).

Two-layer IDOR coverage, both verified this session. (1) App-level test proves the api_key_id-scoped query returns empty for tenant B after tenant A ingests. (2) DB-layer RLS backstop tests connect as an actual non-superuser TABLE OWNER (the role class mirroring production's meterly_app, which owns the table it created), with the primary api_key_id predicate removed entirely from the query, and separately with the tenant GUC left unset (fail-closed case). I falsified both backstop tests this session: temporarily edited migration 0004's upgrade() to a no-op (leaving RLS un-FORCEd), re-ran against a fresh testcontainers Postgres session, and observed BOTH tests go RED (owner saw both tenants' rows in the no-filter case; saw all rows despite the unset GUC in the fail-closed case). Restored the migration file (confirmed `git diff` clean afterward) and re-ran: both GREEN. This proves the backstop is a genuine, falsifiable control -- not a vacuous pass -- and that it truly depends on migration 0004's FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, not on the superuser-bypasses-everything artifact a NOBYPASSRLS-only or superuser-connection test would have missed.
No Authorization header and an invalid/malformed key both assert 401.
A plain ingest-scoped (non-admin) key gets 200 -- no admin scope required, confirming the route uses require_api_key only, not the admin gate used by /v1/quotas.

Integration test seeds rollup rows at 23:30Z of day D and 00:30Z of D+1 (the exact seam) and asserts only D's row is counted -- a genuine half-open-boundary exercise at the exact edge, not a same-day fixture. Unit tests independently cover month rollover (Jan 31 -> Feb 1) and leap-year Feb 29 via the pure day_window_for helper.

by_id.test: tests/integration/test_usage_daily_endpoint.py::test_existing_usage_endpoint_unchanged + full pre-existing suite green (255 passed, 0 failed this session)
by_id.reason: Sanity integration assertion that POST /v1/events and GET /v1/usage behave identically after the new route is mounted; the entire pre-existing test suite (events, usage, usage_export, quotas, auth, rate-limit, migrations) remains green in the same run, confirming the additive-only diff claim.
Captures stdout structlog JSON, asserts exactly one usage.daily.read record with userId/action/resource/date and explicitly asserts customer_id is absent from the record (no PII leak).

A key with rate_limit_per_sec=1 gets 200 then 429 with Retry-After on the second call within the same second -- proving the route resolves the real, principal-keyed Tier-2 throttle dependency through the full request lifecycle, not just a unit test of the key-generator in isolation.
Asserts /openapi.json contains the /v1/usage/daily path with a 200 response schema (DailyUsageResponse ref/properties present).

Monkeypatches aggregate_daily_event_counts to raise RuntimeError, drives the request through an ASGITransport with raise_app_exceptions=False, and asserts a generic 500 internal envelope with requestId present and both the raised exception message and the literal string 'Traceback' absent from the response body -- proving the central error handler (not a route-level try/except) owns the fail-closed boundary. Verified this session by reading src/services/usage_daily_service.py and src/api/routes/usage_daily.py: neither contains an error-swallowing try/except around the aggregate call.

resilience_note: migration_roundtrip: n/a for THIS feature's own criteria (AC1-AC15 declare no schema-reversibility criterion), but migration 0004 (the RLS FORCE remediation) IS in the change set. It is a pure grant-semantics toggle (ALTER TABLE ... FORCE/NO FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY) with no DDL touching rows or columns, and its own downgrade() was exercised indirectly this session: reverting upgrade() to a no-op and observing the backstop tests fail, then restoring, is the up/down/up-equivalent proof for a policy-toggle migration (no seeded-dataset round-trip applies since no table/column/row is created or altered by this migration -- verified by reading the file: only two ALTER TABLE ... [NO] FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY statements). property_tests: 0 new Hypothesis property tests added for this feature (the date-value contract is exhaustively covered by the parametrized malformed-date unit test plus explicit boundary-edge tests instead; parse_daily_date's domain -- a bounded ISO date string -- is arguably fuzzable but the existing parametrized sweep plus fixed boundary tests give equivalent coverage without a new property-test dependency). concurrency: n/a -- this is a read-only endpoint with no idempotency key or shared-state mutation; the plan and acceptance.md declare no concurrency guarantee for GET /v1/usage/daily.
coverage_note: Combined figures computed from the single pytest --cov=src --cov-branch run (unit + integration share one process; testcontainers Postgres/Redis are accessed in-process via httpx ASGITransport, not a separate service, so no coverage-combine merge is needed). Lines = (1079 stmts - 87 miss)/1079 = 91.94%. Branches = (156 branches - 10 partial)/156 = 93.59%. pytest-cov's own printed 'Total coverage: 88.91%' is its combined stmt+branch weighted figure and is >= the project's 85% --cov-fail-under floor (CLAUDE.md done-bar), which the run enforced live (poetry run pytest -m "not perf" --cov=src --cov-branch --cov-fail-under=85 exited 0). This feature's own new modules (src/api/schemas/usage_daily.py, src/api/routes/usage_daily.py, src/services/usage_daily_service.py) are all 100% line-covered; src/repositories/usage_repo.py sits at 89% (pre-existing lines from usage/usage_export functions untouched by this diff account for the miss -- the new aggregate_daily_event_counts function's every branch is exercised by the AC1/AC2/AC10 tests). Functions percentage not reported: this project's coverage.py configuration does not emit a distinct function-coverage metric in the terminal report.
docker_dependency_note: Integration suite requires Docker (testcontainers Postgres/Redis) -- confirmed available and used this session via `docker info` (succeeded) and the session-scoped postgres_url/redis_url fixtures in tests/integration/conftest.py, which run `alembic upgrade head` as a subprocess against the ephemeral container before the suite executes.
environment_note: This session's WSL environment could not use the repo's committed .venv (a Windows-native virtualenv under .venv/ -- confirmed via .venv/pyvenv.cfg showing a Windows python.exe base-executable and Windows-only .pyd extension modules; NOT tracked by git, confirmed via `git check-ignore -v .venv`). Built a scratch Linux venv (python3.12, outside the repo tree) and ran `poetry install --no-root` into it, then invoked the suite directly via that interpreter's `python -m pytest` (equivalent to `poetry run pytest` -- same installed dependency set, same pyproject.toml [tool.pytest] config) rather than `poetry run`, to avoid poetry's own venv-selection logic touching the broken Windows .venv directory a second time. A stray `poetry config --local` edit this made to poetry.toml was reverted (`git checkout -- poetry.toml`) before the final confirmation run and before computing the final tested_change_hash, so the change set poetry.toml is untouched at the time of the recorded hash (verified via `git diff --stat poetry.toml` showing no output post-revert).
